# Code of Conduct

## Context

This project is maintained by a single individual (`@klodr`). Today's
"community" is one person, so an elaborate enforcement ladder would mostly
describe me fighting with myself. The text below is intentionally minimal
and will be extended — adopting [Contributor Covenant](https://www.contributor-covenant.org/)
in full is the plan — as the project grows a second maintainer or a
regular contributor base.

## Expectations

When interacting on this project's GitHub issues, pull requests,
discussions, or any other public surface:

- **Be decent.** No harassment, no personal attacks, no discrimination on
  the basis of race, ethnicity, gender, sexual orientation, disability,
  age, religion, or any other protected characteristic.
- **Stay on topic.** Technical disagreement is welcome; abusive language,
  threats, sexualised content, or doxxing are not.
- **Credit your sources.** If you submit code or prose derived from
  elsewhere, say so.
- **No spam.** Commercial promotion unrelated to the project's scope is
  out of place.

## Reporting

If someone's behaviour on this project's surface is not acceptable,
contact the maintainer privately via the channel listed on the
[@klodr GitHub profile](https://github.com/klodr). Include the details
(link, screenshot, context). Reports are handled confidentially.

The maintainer's response will, depending on severity, range from a
private request to stop, to muting the issue thread, to blocking the
account from the repository.

## Scope

This applies to contributions and conversations on GitHub under this
repository, and to any communication where the participant is presenting
themselves as a member of this project's community.

## Extending this document

If the project grows past a single maintainer, this file will be replaced
with [Contributor Covenant 3.0](https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/3/0/)
verbatim, which provides a proper enforcement ladder and governance
structure. Until then, minimalism is the honest choice.
